method: steel rule or tape across longest face
notes: +/-1/32 in is generous; CNC profile easily holds +/-0.010

method: compare to 1/4-radius gauge or by eye
notes: radius set in CAM; will be exact unless bit is off

method: depth gauge or calipers on deepest letter
notes: if over 0.125, the back face is at risk; if 0 the bit didn't engage

method: backlight the sign with a phone flashlight; look at the back face
notes: if any light shows through, the V depth was too deep — sign is salvageable as a back-lit piece but not as designed

method: measure left margin and right margin from outermost letter strokes; should match
notes: font kerning means perfect math centering is not perfect optical centering; eyeball wins

method: measure from top of stock to top of caps; should be 1.500 in
calipers center-to-center on the wide ends
notes: this dimension matters for mounting; if off, screws won't both engage
too deep risks breakthrough on V-carve; too shallow won't hold the screw head

target: #8 screw head fits wide end, locks in narrow
no nub felt sliding fingernail along edge
no fuzz, no pencil marks, uniform dull surface

method: raking light, both faces and edges
notes: endgrain on plywood drinks finish — may need an extra coat on edges

target: no runs, no sags, no orange peel

method: hang on installed screws, push corners
notes: if rocking, add the optional spacer blocks (p003)
